title: Unlocking India's CDR Potential: A Roadmap for High-Permanence Carbon Dioxide Removal
excerpt: This comprehensive roadmap outlines the deployment potentials, socio-economic benefits, and regulatory requirements for scaling biochar, enhanced rock weathering, and direct air capture in India.
author: CRIA & Academic Partners

title: CCTS Implementation: Recommended Framework for Allocation of Intensity Targets
excerpt: A policy brief submitted to the Ministry of Power, suggesting baseline benchmarks and equity-focused allocation mechanisms for the phase-1 of the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme.
author: CMAI Secretariat

title: Co-benefits of Biochar Application in Smallholder Indian Agriculture
excerpt: Analyzing soil moisture retention, microbial activity increases, and fertilizer reduction savings in rainfed farming systems using biochar soil amendments.
author: CRIA Research Network

title: Bilateral Carbon Trading under Article 6.2: Opportunities for Indian Project Developers
excerpt: A comprehensive primer explaining the mechanism of ITMOs, the role of corresponding adjustments, and the current bilateral agreements between India and partner nations.
author: CMAI Advisory Group

title: Assessing the Integrity of Nature-Based Solutions in India's Carbon Portfolio
excerpt: A deep dive into additionality, permanence, and social safeguards in afforestation and reforestation projects in the Western Ghats and Himalayan regions.
author: CRIA Advisory Council
